mnemonics.title: Paxos Roles Memory
mnemonics.content: PAL: Proposer (suggests), Acceptor (decides), Learner (learns the decision)

mnemonics.title: Consensus Properties
mnemonics.content: AVT: Agreement, Validity, Termination - remember to AVoid Trouble by meeting all three!

mnemonics.title: Message Ordering Types
mnemonics.content: FIFO: First In First Out (sender order); CAUSAL: Happens-Before preserved; TOTAL: Everyone sees same sequence

acronyms.usage: Atomic commitment protocol for distributed transactions - Prepare then Commit/Abort
Used in database systems to ensure all-or-nothing transaction commitment across multiple nodes
Message ordering where sender's messages maintain their send order at receivers
Consensus algorithm named after a legislative system in the island of Paxos

acronyms.expansion: View Management - maintaining consistent group membership across processes

visualTips.visualization: Imagine two parallel timelines for each sender (A and B). In FIFO: A's messages come in order, B's come in order, but A1 can come after B1. In Causal: if A2 depends on B1, then B1 must be delivered first. In Total: everyone sees A1,B1,A2,B2 in exactly same order.

Think of a parliament with proposers (bills), acceptors (voters), and learners (citizens watching). Proposer sends prepare (campaign), Acceptors promise to listen, then Proposer sends accept (vote), Acceptors approve, Learners announce law.
Imagine 4 generals surrounding a city. 1 may be traitor. To attack, all loyal generals must agree on same plan despite the traitor possibly sending different messages to different generals.
Picture people doing a group wave in a stadium. Everyone starts and finishes the wave in the same 'round' - messages delivered in same communication step to all active members.

analogies.analogy: Like a WhatsApp group - one message goes to all members, everyone sees the same messages, members can join/leave, and the group admin manages membership.
Like a synchronized group photo - either everyone is in the photo or no one is. You can't have some people in the photo and others not.
Like a jury reaching a verdict - all must agree on the same decision (agreement), the decision must be based on evidence presented (validity), and they must eventually reach a decision (termination).
Like all students receiving exam papers in the same question order - if student A sees Q1 then Q2, student B must also see Q1 then Q2.
